sources.notes: Alice removes a nonempty proper subset of points, gains removed costs, and Bob minimizes the bounding rectangle perimeter of the remaining points.
Official code uses a 4-bit DP over which bounding-box extremal coordinates of the remaining points have been fixed.
Used to confirm the key property that Bob only needs at most four remaining points determining min/max x/y.

annotation.summary: Compute the optimal score when Alice can remove points for cost and Bob chooses the minimum axis-aligned bounding rectangle around the remaining points.
annotation.constraints: Total n is 3e5, but the rectangle perimeter depends only on four extremal coordinates.
annotation.core_idea: Bob's rectangle is determined by min x, max x, min y, and max y of the remaining points, so the remaining set can be represented by at most four witness points. Alice gets the cost of all removed points, so keeping a point loses its cost but may contribute to one or more of the four extremal coordinates. A 4-bit DP processes points and decides which subset of the four extrema each kept point realizes; skipped points contribute their cost to Alice.
tricks: bounding box determined by four extrema

solution_variants.name: four-extrema-bitmask-dp
solution_variants.summary: DP over the four bounding-box extrema, assigning each kept point to a nonempty subset of still-uncovered extrema.

tags.evidence: The state is a 4-bit mask of which rectangle extrema have already been assigned to kept points.
The payoff depends on the perimeter of an axis-aligned bounding rectangle determined by extremal coordinates.
An arbitrary remaining point set is reduced to the four-coordinate extremal witness state.
